title: Hansenia weberbaueriana
intro: Hansenia weberbaueriana is a flowering plant species in the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the Carrot or Parsley family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds and infections. The bioactive constituents of Hansenia weberbaueriana include alkaloids, phenolic compounds, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the most common herbal preparations made with Hansenia weberbaueriana include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support the immune system and alleviate symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
taxonomy: Hansenia weberbaueriana is a plant species classified within the family Boraginaceae. This family is a part of the order Lamiales and is commonly known as the borage or forget-me-not family.
